meta.title: Morteza Pourmirzai | Where Nature Meets Code
meta.description: Morteza Pourmirzai — wildlife conservation practitioner, founder of Sarvin Wildlife Conservation, and digital infrastructure developer. Where Nature Meets Code.

about.title: Two Worlds, One Person
about.bio: I am Morteza Pourmirzai, a wildlife conservation practitioner and founder of Sarvin Wildlife Conservation. My work focuses on the protection of threatened species—particularly large carnivores such as the Asiatic cheetah and leopard—as well as habitat management and restoration. I take an approach that integrates science, community engagement, and practical policy implementation in conservation projects. Previously, I served as CEO of the Iranian Cheetah Society, where I was involved in a range of field, research, and cross-sector initiatives. Alongside my conservation work, I have a strong background in technical infrastructure, including server management, web optimization, and system development. This combination allows me to design and support projects that are both scientifically grounded and technically robust.
about.bio2: I believe effective conservation depends on the integration of knowledge, technology, and human collaboration—and I aim to bring these elements together in everything I do.

timeline.text: Began environmental studies and volunteer conservation work
Field projects with the Iranian Cheetah Society in Touran & Miandasht
Became CEO of the Iranian Cheetah Society
Presented at the Asia Conservation Congress, Nepal
